note: Road-trip building blocks. type: national_park | national_monument | state_park | town | cultural | scenic. corridor groups them by approach route. dwell = suggested time in hours. Distances are approximate driving miles from Medora, ND.

destinations.name: Theodore Roosevelt National Park — South Unit
destinations.blurb: The scenic loop drive out of Medora — badlands, bison, wild horses and prairie dog towns.

destinations.name: Theodore Roosevelt National Park — North Unit
destinations.blurb: Quieter, more rugged unit off US-85 — the Cannonball Concretions and long views of the Little Missouri.

destinations.name: Painted Canyon Overlook
destinations.blurb: Your first wide view of the Badlands, right off I-94. The easiest hello (and goodbye) there is.

destinations.name: Elkhorn Ranch Site
destinations.blurb: TR's 'home ranch,' where nothing is left standing and the going is the point.

destinations.name: Devils Tower National Monument
destinations.blurb: The nation's first national monument — an 867-foot igneous column above the Belle Fourche River.

destinations.name: Mount Rushmore National Memorial
destinations.blurb: Four presidents carved into the granite of the Black Hills — TR among them.

destinations.name: Wind Cave National Park
destinations.blurb: The first national park created to protect a cave — TR signed it in 1903 — with bison and elk above ground.

destinations.name: Badlands National Park (SD)
destinations.blurb: South Dakota's dramatic eroded spires and mixed-grass prairie along the Loop Road.

destinations.name: Yellowstone National Park
destinations.blurb: Old Faithful, the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone, and the world's first national park.

destinations.name: Grand Teton National Park
destinations.blurb: The Teton range over the Snake River — a float trip and some of the best skyline in the Rockies.

destinations.name: Glacier National Park
destinations.blurb: Going-to-the-Sun Road, alpine lakes and the Crown of the Continent.

destinations.name: Knife River Indian Villages NHS
destinations.blurb: Earthlodge village sites of the Hidatsa and Mandan on the Missouri — where Sakakawea lived.

destinations.name: Fort Abraham Lincoln State Park
destinations.blurb: Custer's last post before the Little Bighorn, plus the reconstructed On-A-Slant Mandan village.

destinations.name: Custer State Park
destinations.blurb: 71,000 acres in the Black Hills — the Wildlife Loop's free-roaming bison herd and the Needles Highway.

destinations.name: Sully Creek State Park
destinations.blurb: Riverside campground and the Maah Daah Hey trailhead, just south of Medora.

destinations.name: Little Missouri State Park
destinations.blurb: Some of the most rugged badlands in the state — best explored on horseback.

destinations.name: Deadwood, SD
destinations.blurb: A restored Gold Rush town — Seth Bullock's grave, Main Street gaming, and Wild Bill's saloon.

destinations.name: Cody, WY
destinations.blurb: Buffalo Bill's town and the Buffalo Bill Center of the West — the Smithsonian of the West.

destinations.name: Spearfish Canyon
destinations.blurb: A limestone canyon of waterfalls and pines — the prettiest drive in the northern Black Hills.

destinations.name: Beartooth Highway
destinations.blurb: US-212 climbs to nearly 11,000 feet — 'the most beautiful drive in America,' per Charles Kuralt.

destinations.name: The Enchanted Highway
destinations.blurb: 32 miles of the world's largest scrap-metal sculptures, running south from I-94 to Regent.

destinations.name: Dickinson Museum Center
destinations.blurb: Badlands Dinosaur Museum with mounted skeletons — an easy stop on the way in from the east.
